starry_unigraph.backends package

class starry_unigraph.backends.CTDGFeatureRoute(route_type: 'str', world_size: 'int', replicated_memory: 'bool' = True)[source]

Bases: object

describe() → dict[str, Any][source]
exchange(ctx: DistributedContext, node_ids: torch.Tensor, values: torch.Tensor, async_op: bool = False) → tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor] | AsyncExchangeHandle[source]

Exchange node features with all peers via all-to-all.

Args:

ctx: DistributedContext with rank / world_size. node_ids: Global node IDs to exchange, shape [N]. values: Feature values to exchange, shape [N, D]. async_op: If True, return an AsyncExchangeHandle instead of blocking.

Returns:

If async_op is False: (merged_ids, merged_values) with features from all ranks concatenated. If True: an AsyncExchangeHandle to call .wait() on later.

replicated_memory: bool = True
route_type: str
world_size: int
class starry_unigraph.backends.CTDGLinkPredictor(num_nodes: int, hidden_dim: int, edge_feat_dim: int, dim_time: int = 100, num_head: int = 2, dropout: float = 0.1, att_dropout: float = 0.1)[source]

Bases: Module

Temporal link prediction module with transformer attention.

Combines a TemporalTransformerConv for computing node representations from sampled neighborhoods, with an edge predictor that scores (src, dst) and (src, neg_dst) pairs.

Args:

num_nodes: Total number of nodes in the graph. hidden_dim: Node memory / representation dimension. edge_feat_dim: Edge feature dimension. dim_time: Time encoding dimension (default 100). num_head: Number of attention heads (default 2). dropout: Dropout rate for output projection. att_dropout: Dropout rate for attention weights.

Forward signature:

output = predictor(src_conv, dst_conv, neg_conv)
# Returns CTDGModelOutput with .pos_logits and .neg_logits
forward(src_conv: Tensor, dst_conv: Tensor, neg_conv: Tensor) → CTDGModelOutput[source]

Defines the computation performed at every call.

Should be overridden by all subclasses.

Note

Although the recipe for forward pass needs to be defined within this function, one should call the Module instance afterwards instead of this since the former takes care of running the registered hooks while the latter silently ignores them.

training: bool
class starry_unigraph.backends.CTDGMemoryBank(num_nodes: int, hidden_dim: int, device: str, mailbox_slots: int = 1, edge_feat_dim: int = 0, rank: int = 0, world_size: int = 1, async_sync: bool = True, node_parts: torch.Tensor | None = None)[source]

Bases: object

Per-node memory + K-slot mailbox.

In distributed mode each rank only allocates storage for its own partition (num_local_nodes nodes). Global node IDs are mapped to local IDs via global2local. Nodes not owned by this rank use a temporary CPU buffer (remote_memory) that is filled by async sync operations.

In single-rank mode num_local_nodes == num_nodes and the mapping is the identity, so all existing call sites work unchanged.

async_sync: bool = True
describe() → dict[str, Any][source]
device: str
edge_feat_dim: int = 0
gather(node_ids: Tensor) → Tensor[source]

Read memory vectors for the given global node IDs.

Args:

node_ids: 1-D tensor of global node IDs (any device).

Returns:

Tensor [len(node_ids), hidden_dim] on self.device. Nodes not owned by this rank get zero vectors.

gather_mailbox(node_ids: Tensor) → Tensor[source]

Legacy single-slot gather (slot 0).

hidden_dim: int
property last_update: Tensor
mailbox_slots: int = 1
node_parts: torch.Tensor | None = None
num_nodes: int
rank: int = 0
read_mailbox(node_ids: Tensor) → Tensor[source]

Read K-slot mailbox for the given global node IDs.

Args:

node_ids: 1-D tensor of global node IDs.

Returns:

Tensor [len(node_ids), K, slot_width] on self.device.

read_mailbox_ts(node_ids: Tensor) → Tensor[source]

Return mailbox timestamps [M, K] on self.device.

submit_async_mail_sync(ctx: DistributedContext, node_ids: Tensor, mail_slots: Tensor, mail_ts: Tensor) → None[source]

No-op for single rank; distributed path mirrors memory sync.

submit_async_memory_sync(ctx: DistributedContext, node_ids: Tensor, values: Tensor, timestamps: Tensor) → None[source]

Submit an async distributed memory sync for remote nodes.

Packs updated memory for nodes owned by other ranks and sends via all_to_all_single. In single-rank mode, falls back to a local memory update. Uses CTDGHistoricalCache (if available) to skip syncs for nodes that haven’t changed significantly.

Args:

ctx: DistributedContext with rank / world_size. node_ids: Global node IDs with updated memory, shape [N]. values: Updated memory vectors (detached), shape [N, D]. timestamps: Update timestamps, shape [N].

sync_updates(ctx, node_ids, values, timestamps)[source]
wait_pending_syncs() → None[source]

Block until all pending async memory and mailbox syncs complete.

Drains _pending_memory_syncs and _pending_mail_syncs, applies received updates to local storage, and updates the historical cache if present.

world_size: int = 1
write_mailbox(src_ids: Tensor, dst_ids: Tensor, src_mem: Tensor, dst_mem: Tensor, edge_feat: Tensor, timestamps: Tensor) → None[source]

Write new messages into the mailbox for src and dst nodes.

Each message slot contains [src_mem || dst_mem || edge_feat] concatenated. Messages are written in timestamp order using a circular buffer (next_mail_pos).

Args:

src_ids: Source node global IDs, shape [B]. dst_ids: Destination node global IDs, shape [B]. src_mem: Source memory before update, shape [B, D]. dst_mem: Destination memory before update, shape [B, D]. edge_feat: Edge features, shape [B, E]. timestamps: Event timestamps, shape [B].

class starry_unigraph.backends.NativeTemporalSampler(dataset: TGTemporalDataset, fanout: list[int], history: int, strategy: str, workers: int = 1)[source]

Bases: object

Multi-hop temporal neighbor sampler backed by the C++ BTS library.

Lazily builds a per-split BTS sampler on first call and caches it.

Args:

dataset: TGTemporalDataset providing the event graph. fanout: Per-layer fanout list (e.g., [10, 10] for 2-hop with 10 neighbors each). history: Number of sampler layers (typically len(fanout)). strategy: Sampling strategy string (e.g., "recent"). workers: Number of BTS sampler worker threads.

Example:

sampler = NativeTemporalSampler(dataset, fanout=[10, 10],
                                history=2, strategy="recent")
output = sampler.sample("train", root_nodes, timestamps)
sample(split: str, root_nodes: Tensor, timestamps: Tensor) → CTDGSampleOutput[source]

Sample temporal neighbors for the given root nodes.

Args:

split: "train", "val", or "test" — determines which temporal graph to sample from. root_nodes: Root node IDs to sample around, shape [R]. timestamps: Timestamps for each root node, shape [R].

Returns:

CTDGSampleOutput with sampled nodes, edges, and BTS blocks.

Raises:

RuntimeError: If the native BTS sampler is not available.

class starry_unigraph.backends.TGTemporalDataset(root: str | Path, name: str, split_ratio: dict[str, float] | None = None, config: dict | None = None)[source]

Bases: object

Temporal graph dataset with train/val/test splitting.

Loads raw temporal events via load_raw_temporal_events(), stores them as flat tensors (src, dst, ts, edge_feat), and provides batched iteration with distributed sharding.

Args:

root: Root directory containing dataset folders. name: Dataset name (subdirectory under root). split_ratio: Optional custom split ratio dict. config: Full session config dict.

Example:

dataset = TGTemporalDataset("/data/raw", "wikipedia")
for batch in dataset.iter_batches("train", batch_size=200):
    print(batch.src.shape)
configure_partition(*, node_parts: torch.Tensor | None, edge_parts: torch.Tensor | None, rank: int, world_size: int) → None[source]
describe() → dict[str, int][source]
iter_batches(split: str, batch_size: int) → Iterator[CTDGDataBatch][source]

Iterate over mini-batches for the given split.

Args:

split: "train", "val", or "test". batch_size: Number of events per batch.

Yields:

CTDGDataBatch instances.

lookup_node_features(node_ids: torch.Tensor, ts: torch.Tensor) → torch.Tensor | None[source]
sampler_graph(split: str) → dict[str, Tensor][source]

Build the temporal neighbor graph for the BTS sampler.

Returns a dict with "row", "col", "eid", "ts" tensors covering all events visible to the given split (train events for "train", train+val for "val", all for "test").

Args:

split: "train", "val", or "test".

Returns:

Dict of CPU tensors suitable for build_temporal_neighbor_block().

split_event_ids(split: str) → Tensor[source]
